content:
📅 Q4 PLANNING

Q4 2026: $580M+ in Year-End Grant Opportunities

📋 November Deadlines:

  • • Nov 5: FedDev Ontario Regional Innovation
  • • Nov 14: USDA Value-Added Producer Grants
  • • Nov 15: PrairiesCan Growth Program
  • • Nov 20: NRC IRAP Accelerated Technology
  • • Nov 30: CanExport Annual Intake

📋 December Deadlines:

  • • Dec 1: Mitacs Accelerate January Cohort
  • • Dec 15: Canada Job Grant (Most Provinces)
  • • Dec 15: SBA Microloan Year-End Close
  • • Dec 31: Clean Growth Hub Programs
  • • Dec 31: SR&ED Fiscal Year Filing

The final quarter of any year is a golden window for grant applicants. In Canada, the federal fiscal year runs April to March — which means October through December is when program officers have the clearest picture of unallocated budgets. In the US, the new fiscal year starting October 1 means fresh funding pools. Either way, Q4 offers some of the best odds you'll see all year.

🔍 Why Year-End Is the Best Time to Apply

+15%

Higher Approval Rates

Q4 vs Q1/Q2 average — agencies push to allocate remaining budgets

-30%

Less Competition

Most businesses delay until January — fewer applicants in the pool

2x

Faster Decisions

Program officers expedite reviews before fiscal year milestones

📅 November 2026: Full Deadline Calendar

FedDev Ontario — Regional Innovation Ecosystem

NOV 5

Program Details:

  • • Funding: Up to $500,000
  • • Type: Non-repayable contribution
  • • Match: 50% cost sharing
  • • Duration: Up to 3 years

Best Candidates:

  • • Innovation hubs and accelerators
  • • Collaborative R&D projects
  • • Industry-academic partnerships
  • • Southern Ontario companies

Success Data:

  • • Approval rate: 28%
  • • Average award: $340,000
  • • GTA: 45% of approvals
  • • Manufacturing sector: highest rate

PrairiesCan Growth Program

NOV 15

Program Details:

  • • Funding: $50,000 – $500,000
  • • Type: Non-repayable grant
  • • Provinces: AB, SK, MB
  • • Focus: Business scaling and trade

Eligible Activities:

  • • Product commercialization
  • • Market diversification
  • • Export capacity building
  • • Technology adoption for scale

Insider Tips:

  • • Alberta energy diversification: +20% score
  • • Saskatchewan ag-tech: highest approvals
  • • Manitoba: least competitive province
  • • First-time applicants: 35% success rate

NRC IRAP — Accelerated Technology Stream

NOV 20
  • • Funding: Up to $1,000,000
  • • Covers: 80% of eligible R&D costs
  • • Eligibility: Canadian-incorporated SMEs with <500 employees
  • • Approval Rate: 45% for returning clients
  • • New Applicant Rate: 22%
  • • Average Award: $385,000
  • • Pro Tip: Call your regional IRAP advisor FIRST
  • • Timeline: 6–10 weeks to decision
  • • Best Sectors: AI/ML, biotech, cleantech, advanced manufacturing

📅 December 2026: Year-End Deadlines

Mitacs Accelerate — January 2027 Cohort (Dec 1)

RESEARCH
  • • Subsidy: 50% of intern salary (up to $15,000 per 4-month term)
  • • Who: Graduate students and postdocs (any discipline)
  • • Duration: 4–16 months
  • • Partner: Must partner with a university professor
  • • Approval Rate: 67% (high — Mitacs is actively expanding)
  • • Best For: Companies needing R&D talent at subsidized cost

Canada Job Grant — Provincial Close (Dec 15)

TRAINING
  • • Grant: Up to $10,000 per employee per year
  • • Employer Pays: 1/3 of training costs
  • • Government Pays: 2/3 of training costs
  • • Eligible Training: External courses, certifications, workshops
  • • Approval Rate: 72% (very high)
  • • Note: Each province administers separately — check your province's exact deadline

SR&ED Tax Credit — Fiscal Year Filing (Dec 31)

TAX CREDIT
  • • Credit: 35% for CCPCs, 15% for others
  • • Deadline: 18 months after fiscal year-end
  • • If your fiscal year ended June 2025: FILE BY DEC 31, 2026
  • • Average Claim: $85,000 for small businesses
  • • Audit Rate: ~20% of claims are reviewed
  • • Pro Tip: Use a specialized SR&ED consultant — they increase claim values by 40% on average

🗓️ Q4 Application Timeline Planner

Week Action Programs to Target Hours Needed
Oct 15–21 Finalize November applications, gather documents FedDev Ontario, PrairiesCan 15–20 hrs
Oct 22–31 Submit November apps, begin December research IRAP, CanExport 10–15 hrs
Nov 1–15 Submit remaining November apps, draft December Mitacs, Canada Job Grant 15–20 hrs
Nov 16–30 Finalize December submissions, SR&ED documentation SR&ED, Clean Growth Hub 20–30 hrs
Dec 1–15 Submit all remaining applications Canada Job Grant, SBA Microloan 10–15 hrs
Dec 16–31 File SR&ED, prepare Q1 2027 pipeline SR&ED filing, Q1 research 15–20 hrs

💡 Year-End Grant Strategy: Expert Advice

✅ DO in Q4:

  • • Pipeline 3–5 applications — success rates are 15–20%, so volume is your friend
  • • Call program officers — they're often more available and helpful in Q4
  • • Leverage fiscal pressure — mention project readiness and quick deployment timelines
  • • Prepare Q1 2027 drafts — use December downtime to get ahead
  • • File SR&ED if eligible — this is free money most businesses leave unclaimed

❌ DON'T in Q4:

  • • Don't rush a bad application — a weak Q4 submission can hurt future applications to the same agency
  • • Don't apply to everything — focus on 2–3 programs where you have the strongest fit
  • • Don't ignore matching requirements — many Q4 grants need 1:1 matching you need to budget for
  • • Don't wait for January — Q1 opens with full competition; Q4 has thinner applicant pools
  • • Don't forget provincial programs — they often have higher approval rates than federal

📅 BUILD YOUR Q4 GRANT PIPELINE NOW

Use our AI to instantly match your business to the best Q4 opportunities

⏰ Don't wait — most Q4 deadlines don't reopen until Q1 2027
